title: Homemade Hash Browns
summary: Making hash browns hungover might seem like a big fuss, so instead of making 12 little fiddly hashy b’s, just make one massive crispy fella. Dead easy.

method.description: Peel and coarsely grate the potatoes. This works best with a food processor with a grater attachment if you’ve got one, but a box grater will work just fine! I’d reach for the food processor if the hangover is anything above a 6/10. Try to make the spud strands as long as you can.
Rinse the potato 3 or 4 times in cold water to remove excess starch and dry thoroughly with a salad spinner or clean tea towels.
Set a large, non-stick frying pan over a medium heat and add 5-6 tbsp of olive oil. Add the potatoes in an even layer, press down into a big hash brown shape and season thoroughly.

Cook for 10-15 mins until brown and crisp and flip out onto a plate. Add another 1-2 tbsp of oil to the pan and return the hash brown, uncooked-side down. Cook for another 10-15 mins until full crisp and cooked through. Chop that big crispy hash brown into chunks and break out the sauce. Get stuck in.

chefs.name: Mob
chefs.summary: Mob is on a mission to instil a love of cooking in everyone, because we believe in the positive impact that it can have on people's lives.
